delta.description: with_skill 100% vs without_skill 88.9% — delta from setup eval only: baseline used useTranslations in a Server Component, confusing server/client distinction

analyst_notes: translator-nextintl-setup: WITH wins on server-vs-client-distinction. Baseline page.tsx used useTranslations (client hook) in a Server Component — this is technically incorrect but went unnoticed without the skill's explicit guidance.
translator-plurals-variables: TIED 4/4. ICU format, =0 case, interpolation, t.rich() are all within baseline knowledge for next-intl. No skill advantage here.
The server-vs-client distinction is the key gap: baseline knows the APIs but doesn't always apply them to the right context. The skill's explicit table and rule makes this reliable.
Consider harder assertions for iteration-2: global.d.ts type safety, generateStaticParams in [locale]/layout, hreflang in generateMetadata alternates.
Baseline is faster (51.7s vs 69.9s) because skill adds more documentation and tables. The quality improvement is real but narrow on current assertions.
